---
title: "aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d0 — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-04
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [34, 22, 22, 22]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot, A-site / P-site / E-site left to right, of per-amino-acid log2 fold-change (median day_10 / median day_0) in whole-transcriptome occupancy. Within each panel the 20 amino acids are sorted by log2 fold-change high to low; bars are coloured by amino-acid physicochemical class (acidic, basic, hydrophobic, neutral, polar; see legend). Y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.21 to +0.22; bars above zero are day_10-enriched, below zero are day_10-depleted.](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/composite/APE_aa_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each panel ranks the 20 amino acids by signed log2 fold-change at one ribosome site (A, P, E): a bar above zero means more day_10 occupancy than day_0 at that site, below zero means less. No significance markers are drawn on the bars.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d0.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d5_vs_d0.csv`
  - `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d5_vs_d0.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U (two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum), day_10 vs day_0
- **Design:** n=4 day_10 vs n=4 day_0 (each n=4 arm pools 2 BWM + 2 control replicates within the timepoint); two-sided Mann-Whitney U on per-(site, amino acid) occupancy; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site is its own family of 20 amino-acid tests); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_day_10 / median_day_0).

### Family-level caveats

- **mw-floor-blocking** — the Mann-Whitney two-sided p-floor for n=4 vs n=4 is exactly 0.02857; with per-site BH families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon), clearing FDR<0.05 is mathematically near-impossible unless ~every test ties at the floor.
- **condition-pooled-confound** — n=4 per timepoint is built by pooling 2 BWM + 2 control replicates (`rep_to_timepoint`); if BWM and control diverge in their time response, pooling masks divergent signals. All 6 members share this design.
- **n=4-low-power** — n=4 vs n=4 Mann-Whitney has very low power; nulls across all 6 files are weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline** — Headlines must explicitly state min raw p ~= 0.0286 and explain why "no FDR hits" here is uninformative, not a biological negative.

---

## Headline

0 of 60 day_10-vs-day_0 amino-acid tests are significant at FDR<0.05; minimum adjusted p is 0.2857. Four cells reach raw p<0.05, and all four sit exactly at the n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney floor (0.02857) — the smallest p the test can return at this N. The absence of FDR hits is therefore a structural floor-blocking outcome, not a biological negative. This is a coordinated floor-limited null. As nominal-only exploratory leads: the largest-magnitude floor cells are P-site C (`log2_FC` +0.1660, enriched in day_10) and P-site I (-0.0955, depleted); the largest-magnitude cell overall, P-site K (-0.2094), sits above the floor (raw p 0.1143).

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median day_10 / median day_0): positive means day_10-enriched, negative means day_10-depleted. `p_adj` is the Mann-Whitney p after BH correction within each site's family of 20 amino acids (the raw Mann-Whitney p per cell is summarised under Numbers at a glance; min raw p = 0.0286 = the n=4 floor). Each site is split into one sub-table per direction. No row reaches FDR<0.05, so every row below is a non-significant lead; within each sub-table the rows are the up-to-5-per-direction amino acids selected by smallest raw p, displayed by `|log2_FC|` descending. Rows flagged `nominal-only` have raw p < 0.05, and all four of them sit exactly at the n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney floor (0.02857), so their raw significance is the maximum the test can produce at this N, not evidence of a strong effect.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---          |
| M (methionine) | +0.1381            | 0.2857               |              |
| F (phenylalanine) | +0.1290         | 0.3810               |              |
| E (glutamate)  | +0.0782            | 0.2857               | nominal-only |
| S (serine)     | +0.0749            | 0.2857               | nominal-only |
| R (arginine)   | +0.0694            | 0.3810               |              |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| W (tryptophan) | -0.1350            | 0.7473               |      |
| P (proline)    | -0.1266            | 0.7473               |      |
| Q (glutamine)  | -0.0978            | 0.7473               |      |
| K (lysine)     | -0.0929            | 0.2857               |      |
| G (glycine)    | -0.0472            | 0.5714               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---          |
| E (glutamate)  | +0.1775            | 0.4571               |              |
| C (cysteine)   | +0.1660            | 0.2857               | nominal-only |
| D (aspartate)  | +0.1547            | 0.9714               |              |
| S (serine)     | +0.0409            | 0.3810               |              |
| N (asparagine) | +0.0356            | 0.9714               |              |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---          |
| K (lysine)     | -0.2094            | 0.4571               |              |
| I (isoleucine) | -0.0955            | 0.2857               | nominal-only |
| G (glycine)    | -0.0505            | 0.9714               |              |
| H (histidine)  | -0.0392            | 0.6667               |              |
| Q (glutamine)  | -0.0303            | 0.9714               |              |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| S (serine)     | +0.2177            | 0.5714               |      |
| L (leucine)    | +0.1148            | 0.6667               |      |
| M (methionine) | +0.0968            | 0.6667               |      |
| C (cysteine)   | +0.0803            | 0.6667               |      |
| T (threonine)  | +0.0334            | 0.6667               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| P (proline)    | -0.1301            | 0.8571               |      |
| K (lysine)     | -0.0864            | 0.5714               |      |
| G (glycine)    | -0.0846            | 0.8831               |      |
| I (isoleucine) | -0.0548            | 0.8831               |      |
| V (valine)     | -0.0476            | 0.9143               |      |

### Flag glossary

- `nominal-only` — raw p < 0.05 but `p_adj` >= 0.05. The row would have been flagged significant if tested in isolation, but did not survive BH-FDR correction across its grouping family. All four `nominal-only` cells in this file sit exactly at the n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney floor (raw p 0.02857), so their raw significance is the test's discrete minimum, not a measure of effect size.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 60 (20 per site, A/P/E)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.2857 (sites A and P)
- `min raw p`: 0.0286 (= the n=4 floor; 4 cells tie here)
- `p_floor`: 0.02857 (theoretical two-sided Mann-Whitney floor for n=4 vs n=4 = 2/C(8,4)). The observed min raw p sits at this floor, so the test maxed out its rank separation at 4 cells yet still cannot clear FDR.
- Per site: site A min `p_adj` 0.2857; site P min `p_adj` 0.2857; site E min `p_adj` 0.5714.

## Biological interpretation

**No day_10-vs-day_0 difference is detectable at amino-acid resolution, and the null is structural rather than biological (concrete).** None of the 60 (site, amino acid) tests clears BH within its 20-amino-acid site family; the minimum adjusted p is 0.2857. The reason is the test's own arithmetic: at n=4 vs n=4 the two-sided Mann-Whitney floor is 0.02857, and even the four cells that reach that floor BH-correct to `p_adj` 0.2857 — clearing FDR<0.05 would require roughly a dozen amino acids at one site to tie at the floor simultaneously. So 0 of 60 is the design's ceiling, not evidence that day_10 and day_0 occupancy are equal. Pooling further limits what the test can see: each n=4 arm mixes 2 BWM + 2 control replicates, so a day-axis shift that points in opposite directions in the two conditions cancels inside the same comparison.

**The only sub-0.05 raw-p cells are four floor ties, split symmetrically across enrichment and depletion (exploratory).** The four `nominal-only` cells are A-site E (+0.0782) and A-site S (+0.0749) enriched, P-site C (+0.1660) enriched, and P-site I (-0.0955) depleted; all sit exactly at the 0.02857 floor, so their raw significance is the maximum the test can return at this N, not a measure of effect strength. They are directional leads to carry into a higher-powered test, not hits. There is no obvious mechanistic anchor tying a cysteine increase and an isoleucine decrease at the P site together, so these are most consistent with the discrete-floor noise the caveats describe; the value of recording them is purely as cells to look for again where N is larger.

**The largest-magnitude cells sit above the floor and are directional only (exploratory).** The file's single largest cell is a P-site lysine depletion at day_10 (`log2_FC` -0.2094), with an E-site serine enrichment (+0.2177) the largest in the other direction; their adjusted p values are 0.4571 and 0.5714 and neither reaches even the raw-p floor. At |log2_FC| ~= 0.2 with n=4, these are the kind of cells that swing on one or two replicates, and neither has a clean amino-acid-level mechanism to lean on here, so they should not be read as findings.

**Site E is flatter than sites A and P (exploratory).** The minimum adjusted p is 0.2857 at both A and P but 0.5714 at E, and E contributes none of the four floor cells. Whatever modest day_10-vs-day_0 modulation the pooled design can register concentrates at the A and P sites. Whether this A/P-over-E pattern is real or an artefact of the floor structure is a question for a higher-powered test on the same time axis, not something this file can settle.

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claim to quote: this file shows no signal at FDR<0.05 for day_10-vs-day_0 amino-acid occupancy with conditions pooled, and the absence is structural (n=4 floor-blocking), not biological. Exploratory patterns worth checking in sister tests: (1) do the floor cells — P-site C enrichment and P-site I depletion, A-site E and S enrichment — reappear with the same direction in the within-condition Fisher `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0`, which holds condition fixed and tests the same time axis at whole-transcriptome N? (2) does the codon-resolution sister `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d0.csv` localise any of them to specific synonymous codons? Noise to ignore: every E-site cell (all adjusted p >= 0.57) and the large-magnitude but non-significant cells (P-site K depletion, `p_adj` 0.4571; E-site S enrichment, `p_adj` 0.5714) that carry magnitude without raw-p support.

## Methods

Columns present: `site`, `amino_acid`, `median_day_10`, `median_day_0`, `log2_FC`, `U_stat`, `p_value`, `p_adj`. The test is a two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum (Mann-Whitney U) comparing day_10 vs day_0 occupancy per (site, amino acid) at n=4 vs n=4. The `U_stat` column and the paired median columns are consistent with that test. The correction column is `p_adj` (BH within each site's 20-feature family, per `merge_global_occupancy_analysis.py`). The test compares the two timepoint distributions per cell; it does not separate the BWM and control replicates that are pooled into each n=4 arm, and at n=4 vs n=4 a single FDR-significant cell would require ~12+ features tied at the floor simultaneously.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **mw-floor-blocking.** The n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney floor is 0.02857; even four cells tied at the floor BH-correct to `p_adj` 0.2857 in a 20-amino-acid per-site family, so "0 of 60 at FDR<0.05" is the design's structural ceiling — do not read it as evidence that day_10 ~= day_0.
- **condition-pooled-confound.** Each n=4 arm pools 2 BWM + 2 control replicates; a BWM-specific and a control-specific time response that point in opposite directions cancel inside the same comparison, so cross-check the within-condition Fisher (`timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0`) before reading any null cell as "no day-axis change."
- **n=4-low-power.** With n=4 vs n=4, a real effect can be missed; treat this null as weakly informative, not strong evidence of equivalence.
- **p-floor-aware-headline.** The headline reports min raw p ~= 0.0286 (the achievable floor) and explains why the FDR null is structural; read the 0 of 60 as "by design," not as a biological negative.

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site day_10-vs-day_0 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2 fold-change high to low (N, M, C, F, E, S, D, R, I, T, V, G, H, L, K, Q, A, P, W, Y); y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.21 to +0.15; bars coloured by amino-acid class (see legend). Largest enrichment at N (~+0.15); largest depletion at Y (~-0.21).](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/individual/site_A_aa_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![P-site day_10-vs-day_0 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2 fold-change high to low (E, C, D, F, S, N, V, Y, A, T, L, W, Q, H, P, G, M, R, I, K); y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.21 to +0.18; bars coloured by amino-acid class. Largest enrichment at E (~+0.18); largest depletion at K (~-0.21).](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/individual/site_P_aa_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![E-site day_10-vs-day_0 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2 fold-change high to low (S, L, Q, M, C, H, W, F, E, T, N, D, A, Y, R, V, I, G, K, P); y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.13 to +0.22; bars coloured by amino-acid class. Largest enrichment at S (~+0.22); largest depletion at P (~-0.13).](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/individual/site_E_aa_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot.png)
